data.title: Netflix Buys Ben Affleck's AI Filmmaking Startup InterPositive
data.description: Streaming giant Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) saw its shares trading steady after announcing the acquisition of InterPositive, a filmmaking technology company co-founded by actor Ben Affleck. The financial details of the deal were not made public. This move reflects Netflix's strategy to enhance its creator-focused artificial intelligence tools for film production. The industry is witnessing a fierce competition between Netflix and Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) to take over Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBD), with Paramount sealing the deal with a bid of $31 per share. Affleck has joined Netflix as a senior advisor along with InterPositive's team. The focus is on developing AI tools to support filmmakers without replacing creative roles. For more details, refer to the complete article available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: GREY2K USA Worldwide and Animal Wellness Action Celebrate House Agriculture Committee Passage of a Ban on Greyhound Racing in America
data.description: Various organizations expressed their gratitude towards a bipartisan group of lawmakers for guiding the Greyhound Protection Act, H.R. 5017, to adoption. GREY2K USA Worldwide and Animal Wellness Action commended the U.S. House Agriculture Committee for this decision in Washington on March 5, 2026. The act aims to protect greyhounds and was successfully adopted following efforts led by these groups.

data.title: Webull Stock Could Soar 100% This Year, Analyst Says
data.description: Shares of Webull Corp. (NASDAQ: BULL) were in the spotlight as Rosenblatt Securities reaffirmed its Buy rating with a revised 12-month price target of 12, down from 15. Analyst Chris Brendler believes heavy marketing spend will impact short-term profitability but positions the digital brokerage for a stronger performance in 2026. Despite closing at $6.07 on Tuesday, suggesting a significant 100% potential upside to the new target, Webull's market cap stands at around $3.2 billion. In the fourth quarter of 2025, Webull reported revenue of $165.2 million, surpassing both Rosenblatt's estimate and the Street consensus.

data.title: Share buyback program
data.description: On March 5, 2026, L'Oréal from Clichy, France, revealed its initiative to engage an investment services provider to acquire its shares as per approval from the Annual General Meeting on April 29, 2025. By the conclusion of June 2026, the cosmetics company plans to execute a buyback scheme for its shares, with a total value reaching 500 million euros and a cap set at 2 million shares to be procured. These repurchased shares will subsequently be nullified.

data.title: Revolution | London Defines the Next Era of Mixer Technology at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2026
data.description: In Rochester, Minnesota, a company called Revolution | London is presenting their e-HYDRIVE™ and xBOLT™ temperature monitoring technologies at CONEXPO 2026. The event serves as a platform to showcase their innovation and expansion in manufacturing.

data.title: Roche announces positive Phase II results for petrelintide, an amylin analog developed for people living with overweight and obesity
data.description: Swiss pharmaceutical company Roche revealed encouraging top-line findings from the Phase II ZUPREME-1 study comparing petrelintide to placebo in 493 individuals dealing with overweight and obesity. The mean BMI of participants was 37 kg/m² in this trial, which had a balanced gender distribution. The primary goal of the research was successfully achieved, showcasing that weekly subcutaneous petrelintide injections (with escalation every fourth week) led to significant weight loss compared to baseline after 28 weeks. This outcome was observed across all five treatment groups when contrasted with the placebo.
Zealand Pharma's petrelintide shows promising in Phase 2 trial for weight management in individuals with overweight/obesity.

data.title: Saber Power Services Acquires Bounds Construction II, LLC
data.description: Houston-based Saber Power Services, LLC, a company backed by Greenbelt Capital Partners, has recently acquired Bounds Construction II, LLC. Bounds, a Tylertown, Mississippi-headquartered firm founded in 2017, specializes in foundation construction for industrial, utility, and energy infrastructure projects. Bounds is known for its quality execution, safety record, and dependable project completion.
